data.body:
PASS-1 review (criterion T11 — data-migration / backfill safety overlay): CLEAN — no T11 findings. T11 is PASS / not-applicable for this plan.

affirmations:
- [T11] Correctly not-applicable per T11's own anti-FP rule ("not-applicable for non-persisted/in-memory changes" / "apply only when the plan changes a schema / persisted format or backfills data"). This plan's deliverables are (1) a project-level disable-the-gate config preference, (2) a written rollback/back-out runbook + deploy-ordering doc, and (3) a spec cross-reference — none of which alters an existing persisted schema or backfills/rewrites existing data, so there is no migration-EXECUTION surface (online/expand-contract, batching/throttling, resumability, dual-write window, partial-failure data-loss) for T11 to evaluate.
- [T11] The new persisted event types the plan touches (the `REVIEW_RESULT` sidecar + the `SIGNATURE`/attestation events) are ADDITIVE, append-only entries to rebar's existing event log — NOT a modification of an existing persisted schema, not a backfill/rewrite of existing rows, and not a blocking DDL on a large table. Tool-grounded: `REVIEW_RESULT` is reducer-IGNORED and compaction PRESERVES it (src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sidecar.py:7-14, src/rebar/reducer/_version.py:44); it is added like the SYNC/PRECONDITIONS precedent. No existing rows are rewritten.
- [T11] The gate DEFAULTS OFF and the cert binds to a head MATERIAL-event commit; pre-existing / in-flight tickets are simply ungated rather than backfilled, so there is deliberately no data-backfill step (the plan's own Scope frames "rollback" as turning the preference off, not migrating data).
- [T11] The genuine residual concern in this neighborhood — old+new coexistence / deploy-ordering for the new event types and mixed-version readers (Scope items 2 & 3 / AC2) — is migration-ROLLOUT/reversibility, correctly the province of T12 (rollout/rollback/reversibility), NOT T11 (migration-EXECUTION safety). Noted only to disambiguate the two overlays; it is NOT a T11 finding.

No checklist items emitted for T11.
